headline: Survey at 10% of target with strong regional concentration and clear expansion mandate.
collection_status: 141 responses collected against 1,350 target (10.4% completion). LINDI dominates with 45% of responses; 7 of 10 regions active but 3 regions (Dar es Salaam, Dodoma, Iringa) absent. Current pace insufficient to meet target without immediate acceleration.
key_findings: Expand KiuFunza is dominant priority (37% of respondents), followed by new programs (21%) and policy integration (20%), indicating stakeholder consensus on scaling over research.
Partner satisfaction high at 4.5/5 but scale readiness critically low at 2.7/5, suggesting capability gaps in systems, training, or infrastructure needed for expansion.

Teacher representation strong (48 KF-lower + 17 KF-upper = 46% of sample) but volunteer/community voices underrepresented (20 volunteers vs. institutional respondents), risking grassroots perspective gaps.
Qualitative feedback emphasizes government coordination, teacher incentives, digital monitoring systems, and inter-stakeholder communication—pointing to systemic barriers beyond program design.
flags: Geographic coverage incomplete: 3 priority regions have zero responses; LINDI over-represents (45% of sample). Sampling bias risks regional strategy misalignment.
Scale readiness score (2.7/5) contradicts expansion appetite—urgent diagnostic needed on bottlenecks (capacity, funding, systems) before scaling commitment.
Collection rate at 10% completion with no clear acceleration plan visible; current trajectory misses target by 1,200+ responses without intervention.
recommendation: Immediately launch targeted collection in underrepresented regions (Dar es Salaam, Dodoma, Iringa) and conduct rapid readiness assessment to diagnose the 2.7/5 scale gap—this will inform whether expansion should proceed or be phased pending systemic strengthening.
